subject: Sports
title: Hawk-Eye: The Robot Umpire
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Hawk-Eye: The Robot Umpire.

hook.text: Was the ball in or out? Hawk-Eye tracks it to within 3.6 millimeters. No argument needed.

buildup.text: Hawk-Eye uses multiple high-speed cameras to triangulate the exact position of a ball in 3D space.

discovery.text: First used in cricket in 2001, it spread to tennis, football (goal-line tech), and baseball.

twist.text: In tennis, player challenges using Hawk-Eye became entertainment themselves—crowds cheer the animation.

climax.text: Technology didn't replace referees—it gave them a safety net for the calls humans simply cannot see.

punchline.text: Cameras see what eyes can't. Hawk-Eye ended arguments with data.

quiz.question: How accurate is Hawk-Eye's ball-tracking technology?
